summary:
The patient is likely suffering from a form of scleroderma where specific skin involvement occurs due to high prevalence reports linking anti-centromere antibody presence directly related primarily towards limited cutaneous forms thereof over broader systemic manifestations as found predominantly within more generalized connective tissue disorders such as Lupus Erythematous or even primary SS cases involving milder autoantibody profiles including SSA/SSB among others but rarely centromeres alone without additional features pointing toward full-blown SC scleroform disease course.
